description_long: This bungalow features a simple rectangular structure with a gable roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The front facade is clad in horizontal wooden planks and includes a pair of symmetrically placed windows flanking a large double door, all framed with dark hardware. The side and rear walls are unadorned, constructed from the same light-colored wood, and lack any windows or doors. The roof extends slightly beyond the walls, providing minimal eaves for rain protection. The base of the structure sits on a narrow brick or stone foundation, elevating it slightly above ground level. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on function and straightforward construction, suitable for use as a small residential or utility building in an urban or suburban setting.
description: A compact wooden bungalow with a gable roof, double front doors, and two symmetrical front windows, finished in light wood and reddish-brown shingles.
description_view_0: Front view showing double wooden doors and two symmetrically placed windows.
description_view_1: Side gable end with no windows or doors, highlighting the triangular roof profile.
description_view_2: Rear view with plain wooden siding and no openings, showing the full width of the roof.
description_view_3: Opposite gable end, identical to the other side, with no windows or doors.
